meta.whyDifferent: Charged Blade is a permanent BasicAttack that cantrips unconditionally and is added to the run deck by Stormblade. Cogspark is the pack's Conjuration-category token (temporary by construction): its cantrip is Cascade-gated, so the deck must weave 1-cost cards between 0-cost Cogsparks to keep replacing them. New decision = sequencing your costs, not just playing the token.
meta.intendedDeck: Conjured by Winding Key / Assembly Line. Chain keyword keeps the chain alive and feeds Mainspring Coil / Stutterbolt; Cascade rewards 0-1-0-1 cost weaving.
meta.powerNotes: 0-cost: 1 dmg + 1 chain (keyword) + conditional cantrip. Zap (0-cost common) = 2 dmg + 1 chain. Trades 1 dmg for a sequencing-gated draw; on-budget for 0-cost common (~2-3 dmg-equiv). Being Conjured, it never bloats the run deck.

cards.name: Gearbite
cards.description:
Deal [damage:2] damage.
If you've already played a Gearbite this turn, deal [damage:6] damage instead.

meta.archetype: echo/copy payoff at common (bridge: copy <-> melee combo)
meta.whyDifferent: Aura of Mind is the only friendly AlreadyPlayedThisTurn user and is a passive Focus aura. Gearbite puts that condition on a damage card itself: the second same-name play this turn triples the hit. New payoff mode for an existing setup - it wants duplicates in deck, conjured copies (copy/Recall/Figmented share cardID), or replays. No shipped attack cares about its own name having been played.
meta.intendedDeck: 2x Gearbite in a tempo shell, or one copy plus copy effects (Fractal Bolt pattern, Recast, conjured duplicates). Plays first as a vanilla 2, second as a 6.
meta.powerNotes: 1-cost common median is 4 dmg. Gearbite is 2 below baseline unconditioned, 6 (+2 over) with a real setup cost (play a second copy the same turn). Conditional overage matches the value of the setup per spec par. AlreadyPlayedThisTurn counts hand-played same-cardID entries (ConditionChecker.AlreadyPlayedThisTurn), so Echo auto-replays do NOT satisfy it - only real second plays.

cards.name: Sprocket Volley
cards.description:
Deal [damage:1] damage twice.
If you played a Conjuration this turn, deal it twice more.

meta.whyDifferent: Wintersprig gates its extra attack on Flow (raw play count). Sprocket Volley uses CategoryPlayedThisTurn=conjuration - a condition ZERO shipped cards use (verified by scan of all 2,525 playConditions+effectConditions). It asks a different question: not 'how many cards' but 'did your engine produce'. First payoff that cares that a Conjuration-category card was PLAYED this turn.
meta.intendedDeck: Conjuration engines (Winding Key, Assembly Line, or any shipped conjurerandom deck). Playing a Cogspark first turns this into 4 hits.
meta.powerNotes: 0-cost: 2 dmg base, 4 dmg conditional. 0-cost common par is 2-3; conditional +2 is paid for by playing a Conjuration first. Multi-hit synergizes with Chain status (+stacks per hit) - intended, and bounded by chain's end-of-turn expiry.

meta.whyDifferent: Stormbolt is a damage spell whose Zaps are a conditional rider (Continuity/Empower). Winding Key is pure engine: no damage, tokens are the whole card, and Empower makes it the first conjuration card that pays you for BANKING energy across turns (energy persists; EnergyHandler.SetEnergyToZero is empty). New decision: hold energy this turn to wind the engine harder next turn.
meta.intendedDeck: Cogspark tempo. Feeds Tickwork Aura (chain per conjure), Sprocket Volley (conjuration played), Assembly Line (play count).
meta.powerNotes: 1 INT for 2 (3 with >4 total energy) Cogsparks = ~2-3 dmg-equiv + 2-3 chain + potential cantrips, delivered slowly (each token costs a play). Comparable to Stormbolt's 4 dmg + conditional Zaps at same cost. Loop-brake: tokens are Conjured (vanish after combat), hand cap 10, and each conjure costs the play tempo it grants.

cards.name: Mainspring Coil
cards.description: Gain Chain equal to the cards you've played this turn ([[actionsPlayed]]).

meta.whyDifferent: Stormcast's chain gain scales with upgrades (deck-building axis); Chain Lightning's with damage rolled (variance axis). Mainspring Coil is the first chain battery that scales with CARDS PLAYED THIS TURN - it rewards sequencing (play it late, after the cheap links) rather than deck investment or luck. Same setup family as Flow, new payoff: chain stacks instead of a fixed rider.
meta.intendedDeck: All-Chain lightning deck as links 4-6 of the turn, converting a long turn into a big Shocking Grasp / Stutterbolt / next-hit bonus.
meta.powerNotes: [[actionsPlayed]] verified in shipped codeLines (Daring_Dash: energy:DEX:[[actionsPlayed]]). Played 4th it gives 4 chain (+1 from its own Chain keyword) for 1 INT - about Stormcast+2 with real sequencing setup; chain still dies at EndTurnPhase (Chain.json), so the value must be spent same turn.

cards.name: Stutterbolt
cards.description: Deal [damage:3] damage. Play a copy for every 3 Chain you have ([[my(status)Chain]]). Breaks your Chain.

meta.whyDifferent: Gathering Storm's playcopy count grows by sitting in hand (patience axis). Stutterbolt's grows with Chain stacks - and it deliberately does NOT have the Chain keyword, so casting it ends the chain it cashes out. Existing chain spenders (Shocking Grasp, Energize) convert chain to damage/energy; none convert chain into PLAYS. New decision: kill your own engine at the right moment.
meta.intendedDeck: Chain decks as the finisher: build 6-9 chain with links, then convert to 3-4 casts of 3 (each still buffed by chain on the first hit).
meta.powerNotes: LOOP-BRAKE (engine card): playcopy re-runs the card's PlayAction lines on each copy, but because Stutterbolt is non-Chain, EndActionStep wipes ALL chain when the first cast resolves - every replay evaluates [[my(status)Chain]]/3 = 0. Self-extinguishing by construction, verified against the Gathering Storm playcopy re-entry pattern (their brake is addtempvalue:-999; mine is the chain wipe rule itself). At 6 chain: 3+3+3 = 9 dmg for 1 INT, minus the chain (+6 next-hit) you gave up - net swing ~3 over baseline with full setup.

meta.whyDifferent: Twinlightning (Echo) double-casts immediately - burst, no decisions. Fractal Bolt uses Figmented, a fully engine-verified keyword (EndActionStep conjures a Conjured, non-Figmented copy to hand) that ZERO shipped player cards carry: the second cast arrives as a real card you must pay 1 INT for, this turn or later. Revives a dormant mechanic as its first shipped user; the copy is also a same-cardID play for Gearbite/Resonance Chamber-style payoffs.
meta.intendedDeck: Echo/copy shell with Resonance Chamber; also a Conjuration producer... NOTE: the Figmented copy is Conjured but Action-category, so it does NOT satisfy CategoryPlayedThisTurn=conjuration - only Cogspark does. Deliberate anti-overlap.
meta.powerNotes: 3 dmg for 1 INT plus a deferred copy that costs full price = 6 dmg for 2 INT total, split across plays. Below Twinlightning's immediate ~8 avg for 1, above it in flexibility/synergy. UNVERIFIED-in-content: no shipped card carries Figmented; mechanics verified in SpellEffects.EndActionStep per GAME-MECHANICS Part III. Loop-brake: the copy drops the Figmented keyword (no infinite lineage) and each generation costs full energy.

cards.name: Tickwork Aura
cards.description: This combat, whenever you Conjure a card, gain 1 Chain.

enchantment.text: Whenever you Conjure a card, gain 1 Chain.

meta.whyDifferent: Conjurer's Boon (Rare, 3 INT) heals when you PLAY a Conjuration. Tickwork Aura triggers on the Conjure event itself (GameTriggers.Conjure - creation, not play; shipped enchantments use trigger 16 only for cost/keyword mutation like Effortless Magic) and pays out in Chain, wiring the conjure archetype directly into the chain archetype. These two archetypes currently don't talk on any shipped card.
meta.intendedDeck: Winding Key / Assembly Line shells that also run chain spenders; makes conjurerandom lightning cards (Innervation, Evocation) chain-positive.
meta.powerNotes: 1 chain per conjure is small and same-turn-perishable (Chain expires EndTurnPhase); the aura does nothing without both engines running. Loop-brake: generates no cards itself - pure conversion, bounded by conjure count and chain expiry. Unique (all Enchantments are).

cards.name: Unbroken Cadence
cards.description: This combat, cards you draw or Conjure gain Chain.

enchantment.text: Cards you draw or Conjure gain Chain.

meta.whyDifferent: Exact structural sibling of Twisted Echoes (monster affix: Draw+Conjure triggers, addkeyword:Echo) - but player-side, granting Chain, and using addtempkeyword so the grant is combat-scoped instead of permanently mutating run-deck containers. Solves the chain archetype's core constraint (any non-Chain card wipes chain, forcing mono-Chain decks): with this down, ANY deck chains. No shipped player card grants Chain to other cards.
meta.intendedDeck: Chain-splash decks: run big non-Chain payoffs (Fireball, anything) inside a chain engine; every card now sustains and grows chain by +1.
meta.powerNotes: +1 chain per card played (~+1 dmg ramp per play, expiring each turn) for 2 INT, Rare - comparable to Twincasting (Rare, 3 total) as a turn-engine. Loop-brake: no card generation; chain still expires at EndTurnPhase; only affects cards entering hand AFTER cast. UNVERIFIED (flagged for sim/QA): that addtempkeyword's card-context inside an enchantment binds to the drawn/conjured card as it does for Twisted Echoes' addkeyword - decompile shows AddtempKeyword(cc,...) falls back to first-card-in-hand when cc is null. Must be play-tested; if binding fails, switch codeLines to the literal Twisted Echoes form addkeyword:Chain and accept combat-only via Conjured/temp filtering.

cards.name: Assembly Line
cards.description: Conjure a free Cogspark for each card you've played this turn ([[actionsPlayed]]).

meta.whyDifferent: Apocalypse scales its conjure count with tempValue accumulated across turns (patience). Assembly Line scales with cards played THIS turn - the combo deck's play count becomes the conjure engine's fuel, closing the loop the other way from Sprocket Volley. No shipped conjure command scales with [[actionsPlayed]].
meta.intendedDeck: Play 4-5 cheap links, then refill the hand with free Cogsparks; with Tickwork Aura down, also +4-5 chain on the spot.
meta.powerNotes: LOOP-BRAKE analysis (this is the pack's scariest engine): Cogsparks it makes each count as plays, so a second Assembly Line conjures more - but generation is bounded by (a) hand cap 10 (DrawCards/conjure respect MAXIMUM_CARDS), (b) each Cogspark must actually be played, costing queue time and granting only 1 dmg + 1 chain, (c) Assembly Line itself costs 2 INT per cast and you only draw so many. No free recursion: the card never conjures copies of ITSELF and Cogspark conjures nothing. Rare at 2 INT for ~4-6 free 0-cost tokens late-turn = ~5 dmg-equiv + chain + cascade draws, within rare cost-2 envelope (5-8).

cards.name: Resonance Chamber
cards.description: This combat, whenever you play a card you've already played this turn, gain 2 Chain and deal [damage:2] damage.

enchantment.text: Whenever you play a card you've already played this turn, gain 2 Chain and deal [damage:2] damage.

meta.whyDifferent: Aura of Mind pays 1 Focus (slow, threshold-6 draw economy) for repeat plays. Resonance Chamber pays in the tempo cluster's native currencies - chain and immediate lightning damage - turning every duplicate, conjured copy, and Figmented replay into a chain link even when the copy itself isn't a Chain card. Repeat-play as a damage engine doesn't exist player-side.
meta.intendedDeck: Copy shell: 2x commons, Fractal Bolt copies, Recast/Recall targets, Cogspark swarms (2nd+ Cogspark each turn triggers it).
meta.powerNotes: Trigger requires a same-cardID second hand-play in one turn - real deck cost. Per trigger: 2 dmg + 2 chain (~4 dmg-equiv same-turn). 2-3 triggers/turn in a dedicated deck = rare-level engine for 1 INT; gated hard by needing duplicates. Loop-brake: generates no plays or cards itself, purely reactive.

cards.name: Overclock
cards.description: Draw 2 cards. Flow ([[actionsPlayed]]/8): Take an extra turn. One Use.

meta.whyDifferent: Shipped extra turns are gated by cost (Timestop, 4 INT flat), resource dump (Brainwarp, 20 Focus), or delve luck (Eternity, Revelation). Overclock is the first gated on raw combo count: Flow 8 means the deck must physically play 8 cards first - exactly what this pack's token engines produce. The extra turn is the reward for the archetype's core activity, not for a parallel resource.
meta.intendedDeck: Any Clockwork shell: chain links + Cogspark swarm reach 8 plays; whiffing still cantrips (draw 2, which itself can find the last links).
meta.powerNotes: LOOP-BRAKE: OneUse - the card is permanently gone after a single cast (EndActionStep), so no extra-turn recursion; the fresh turn also resets [[actionsPlayed]], so even a hypothetical second copy starts from 0. Flow auto-passes on Reliable cards - Overclock is not Reliable and no shipped effect grants Reliable+replay to OneUse cards; noted for sim. Budget: Brainwarp (Rare, 3 INT, ~20-Focus gate) vs this (Legendary, 2 INT, 8-play gate, OneUse) - stricter gate, lower cost, single use.

meta.nearestExisting: Forcewand.json (2 lightning + chain feed); Knuckles.json (Cascade: conjure a copy)
meta.whyDifferent: Forcewand feeds chain stacks; Knuckles self-copies on a cost-weave. The Baton mints the pack's token only when played third-or-later in a turn (Flow 2) - the basic attack becomes the combo FINISHER that banks tomorrow's link, the opposite sequencing decision to Knuckles' opener-weave. Carries Chain so it never breaks the chain it serves.
meta.intendedDeck: Clockwork Cadence combo turns: Cogspark output feeds Winding Key/Assembly Line counters and keeps Mainspring Coil fed.
meta.powerNotes: Statline par: damage 2 at 1 Neutral + Chain keyword (=1 chain stack on resolve, half of Forcewand's feed). Gated Cogspark ~ 1 dmg + 1 chain + Cascade-gated draw of temporary value; needing 2 prior plays prices it at/under Forcewand's unconditional +2 chain.

weaponPowers.name: Ratchetwind Weapon
weaponPowers.description: Use your weapon power to start next combat with a free Cogspark per level (up to 4).

meta.nearestExisting: Thundering Weapon (cd3: conjure:ref:[[myLevel]]:4 Zaps); Quicksilver Weapon (cd2: daggers, free)
meta.whyDifferent: Same shipped chassis, different physics: Zaps are burst damage, Cogsparks are Chain links with Cascade cantrips - the opener seeds the pack's sequencing engine (chain retention + conjured-count payoffs) rather than face damage.
meta.intendedDeck: Any Clockwork deck; four 0-cost links let turn 1 hit Flow thresholds and keep Tickwork Aura/Resonance Chamber counting.
meta.powerNotes: cd3 curve point: conjure-per-level-capped-4 is shipped verbatim at cd3 (Thundering). Cogspark's per-copy value (1 dmg + chain + gated draw) ~ Zap's (2 dmg + chain); on-curve by construction. codeLine is a shipped tier-0 line character-for-character.

weaponPowers.name: Escapement Weapon
weaponPowers.description: Use your weapon power to add the Chain keyword to a card in your deck.

meta.nearestExisting: Coiling Weapon (cd4: upgrade all Chain cards); Psychic Weapon (cd4: addkeywordindeck:psionic + extras)
meta.whyDifferent: Coiling makes existing links bigger; Escapement makes NEW links - it permanently converts any engine card (a 2-cost bomb, a draw spell) into a non-chain-breaking play, directly attacking the archetype's core constraint (any non-Chain card wipes chain stacks).
meta.intendedDeck: Chain decks whose payoff cards break their own chain (Overclock, Assembly Line) - graft Chain onto the payoff and the engine never stalls.
meta.powerNotes: cd4 curve point: permanent deck edits live at cd4 (Coiling/Psychic/Forging). Single lean line, deliberately under Psychic's triple package, because a well-chosen graft compounds every combat. UNVERIFIED: addkeywordindeck ships only with the psionic argument; chain is a legal CardProperties member (Chain=1) on the same enum-parse path - flagged for in-game QA.

weaponPowers.name: Flywheel Weapon
weaponPowers.description: Use your weapon power to start next combat with a clockwork engine of your choice.

meta.nearestExisting: Artificer's Weapon (cd3: addnextbykeyword:equipment:3); Deathdealers Weapon (cd3: addnextcard:3 with three fixed Contract refs)
meta.whyDifferent: The Deathdealers chassis pointed at the pack's own engines: the choice among Winding Key (token faucet), Tickwork Aura (chain counter) and Mainspring Coil (link feed) is a real per-fight read of which axis the next combat needs - not a generic keyword pool pull.
meta.intendedDeck: All Clockwork decks; smooths the archetype's worst failure mode (engine pieces stuck at the bottom of the deck).
meta.powerNotes: cd3 curve point: choice-of-3 start-of-combat card is the modal shipped cd3 payoff (Artificer's/Deathdealers/Explosive/Familiar). All three refs are the pack's own 1-cost C/U cards - materially in line with shipped Contract/Equipment pulls. addnextcard:3 + referenceCard list is shipped-verbatim.
